research_interest_summary: Trustworthy, efficient, and structure-aware foundation models at the intersection of graph learning, geometric deep learning, LLMs, world models, VLMs, VLA systems, and agent systems.
core_objective: Use graph and other explicit structures to improve reasoning, computation, alignment, memory, retrieval, planning, controllability, and long-horizon decision-making in foundation models.

notes_for_search.llm_keywords_usage: Use keywords_for_llm for semantic ranking, prompt writing, and LLM-based paper triage.
notes_for_search.arxiv_keywords_usage: Use keywords_for_arxiv and arxiv_query for arXiv API search. Prefer short phrases and boolean combinations over long composite natural-language queries.
priority_preference: explicit structure such as graphs, relations, memory graphs, knowledge graphs, scene graphs, or structured planners
methods that improve reasoning, efficiency, alignment, safety, controllability, or scientific usefulness
forward-looking work likely to matter in the next 2-5 years
deprioritize: incremental benchmark improvements without conceptual novelty
purely sequential LLM work without structural insight
application-only papers with limited methodological contribution

research_interests.focus: How graph and structured inductive biases improve LLM reasoning, computation, alignment, memory, retrieval, and tool use.
research_interests.arxiv_query: ((ti:"large language model" OR abs:"large language model" OR ti:llm OR abs:llm) AND (ti:"graph" OR abs:"graph" OR ti:"knowledge graph" OR abs:"knowledge graph" OR ti:"graph rag" OR abs:"graph rag" OR ti:"structured memory" OR abs:"structured memory" OR ti:"relational" OR abs:"relational") AND (abs:reasoning OR abs:planning OR abs:alignment OR abs:retrieval OR abs:memory OR abs:"tool use"))

research_interests.focus: How graph and relational structure improve grounding, multimodal reasoning, representation, controllability, and long-horizon prediction in world models and VLMs.
keywords_for_llm: relational inductive bias for world models
research_interests.arxiv_query: ((ti:"world model" OR abs:"world model" OR ti:"vision-language model" OR abs:"vision-language model" OR ti:vlm OR abs:vlm OR ti:multimodal OR abs:multimodal) AND (ti:"graph" OR abs:"graph" OR ti:"scene graph" OR abs:"scene graph" OR ti:"relational" OR abs:"relational" OR ti:"structured" OR abs:"structured") AND (abs:grounding OR abs:prediction OR abs:reasoning OR abs:controllability OR abs:"long-horizon"))

research_interests.focus: Safety, robustness, and trustworthiness in agent systems, especially unlearning, jailbreaking, privacy, and prompt injection in tool-using and multimodal agents.
research_interests.arxiv_query: ((ti:agent OR abs:agent OR ti:"tool-using" OR abs:"tool-using" OR ti:"llm agent" OR abs:"llm agent") AND (abs:safety OR abs:robustness OR abs:alignment OR abs:trustworthy OR abs:secure) AND (abs:unlearning OR abs:jailbreak OR abs:jailbreaking OR abs:"prompt injection" OR abs:"indirect prompt injection" OR abs:privacy))

research_interests.focus: Diffusion- and flow-matching-based methods for faster, lower-latency, and more controllable VLA and world model systems.
research_interests.arxiv_query: ((ti:"vision-language-action" OR abs:"vision-language-action" OR ti:vla OR abs:vla OR ti:"world model" OR abs:"world model") AND (abs:diffusion OR abs:"diffusion policy" OR abs:"flow matching") AND (abs:efficient OR abs:acceleration OR abs:"fast sampling" OR abs:"low latency" OR abs:"real-time" OR abs:robotics OR abs:"embodied"))

research_interests.focus: Structure-aware foundation models and world models for scientific machine learning, especially molecules, proteins, materials, and physics.
research_interests.arxiv_query: ((ti:"scientific machine learning" OR abs:"scientific machine learning" OR ti:"ai for science" OR abs:"ai for science" OR abs:molecular OR abs:protein OR abs:materials OR abs:physics) AND (abs:"foundation model" OR abs:"world model" OR abs:"geometric deep learning" OR abs:graph OR abs:relational) AND (abs:diffusion OR abs:"flow matching" OR abs:dynamics OR abs:simulation))
